title: Thelypteris tottoides
taxonomy: Thelypteris tottoides is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Within the family Thelypteridaceae, Thelypteris tottoides is placed in the genus Thelypteris, which is a genus of relatively small to medium-sized ferns. The genus Thelypteris is further divided into several species, with Thelypteris tottoides being one of them. Thelypteris tottoides is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas.
uses: Thelypteris tottoides has been reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, including being used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
